frame_analyses.description: Needle driver with suture is being prepared.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle grasp is firm and controlled.

frame_analyses.description: Needle is advanced toward the marked entrance site.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Approach to mark looks accurate, slight hesitation noted.

frame_analyses.description: Needle is inserted through the first mark.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Insertion angle could be improved for precision.

frame_analyses.description: Preparing for first throw of the surgeon's knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good use of instrument for knot positioning, maintaining tension.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw completed, securing the knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Tension maintained adequately, no visible slippage.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting the ends of the suture.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Precision maintained while cutting, no suture remains.

knot_assessments.notes: Surgeon's knot executed well with no visible slippage.
Solid second throw with good hand switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Closure achieved with no visible gap.

technique_summary: The intracorporeal suturing was performed with adequate precision and speed. Throws were secure, and hand switches were executed correctly.
improvement_suggestions: Enhance precision during initial suture placement to reduce deviation.
Maintain consistent tension throughout knot tying.
strengths: Effective hand switching during knot tying.
Secure and solid knot formation observed.
